concerns.text: FR-006 mandates linear regression to estimate R² but does not specify how to handle the ordinal nature of 'flare class' (logarithmic scale). Without a mandatory transformation (e.g., log10 flux) or a non-linear model fallback, the R² value is scientifically unverifiable as a valid metric of variance explained.

FR-011 requires a 'hold-out validation strategy' but fails to specify a temporal split (e.g., train on past, test on future). Random k-fold splitting on time-series data causes data leakage, making the 'detection rate' metric unverifiable and the validation invalid.

The assumption regarding 'post-hoc power analysis' (target power ≥0.8) is unverifiable because it lacks a pre-specified effect size assumption (e.g., expected correlation coefficient). Without this, the system cannot determine if N is sufficient, rendering the 'power limitation warning' logic untestable.

FR-007 requires identifying thresholds with 'explicit justification citing community-standard basis' but does not define the specific standard (e.g., NOAA Dst≤-100 nT) as a mandatory input for the justification logic, making the 'explicit justification' subjective and hard to verify automatically.

FR-006 requires VIF calculation but lacks a defined threshold for 'high collinearity' (e.g., VIF > 5) and a mandatory fallback procedure (e.g., Ridge regression or separate univariate models). If VIF is high, the 'variance explained' claim becomes scientifically invalid, yet the spec provides no verifiable path to handle this.

SC-003 measures 'detection rate variation' but does not define the 'detection rate' metric (e.g., True Positive Rate, Precision) or the ground-truth definition of a 'severe storm' within the validation logic. Without a precise formula, the metric is not reproducible.
